alternative_expected_tool_calls.reason: 'Front door' could also refer to the lock state; binary_sensor is the dedicated contact sensor for open/closed status.

metadata.notes: Step 1: HassGetState × binary_sensor. Door device_class — state off = closed. Note: friendly name is 'Front Door' (no 'Contact' suffix), making this an entity_id confabulation risk (binary_sensor.front_door_contact, not binary_sensor.front_door).

utterance: is there motion in the living room

metadata.notes: Retargeted from binary_sensor.hallway_motion (not in medium tier) to binary_sensor.living_room_motion.

alternative_expected_tool_calls.reason: Motion sensor can also detect presence; occupancy sensor (mmWave) provides more reliable sustained presence detection.

metadata.notes: Step 2: Occupancy subtype (device_class: occupancy). 'Is anyone in' maps to occupancy over motion. State is on (occupied). Disambiguation: office has both occupancy and motion sensors.

utterance: is there a leak under the kitchen sink

metadata.notes: Step 2: Moisture subtype (device_class: moisture). 'Leak under the sink' → moisture sensor. State is off (no leak). Natural language → moisture device_class inference.

utterance: is the kitchen smoke detector alarming

metadata.notes: Step 2: Smoke subtype (device_class: smoke). State is off = no smoke detected. Note entity_id is binary_sensor.kitchen_smoke, not binary_sensor.kitchen_smoke_detector.
